## Jan 16, 2026

# A Brutalist Reinterpretation of Digital Excellence: The New Awwwards Trophies

- [![](https://assets.awwwards.com/awards/media/cache/thumb_user_70/avatar/57289/620f55adbdb42025477900.png)**awwwards.**](https://www.awwwards.com/awwwards/)


![A Brutalist Reinterpretation of Digital Excellence: The New Awwwards Trophies](https://assets.awwwards.com/awards/images/2026/01/aat-cs-cover02.jpg)

## The new Awwwards trophies translate the values of contemporary digital design into a physical object through material honesty, structural clarity and a strong architectural presence.

Developed from the very identity of the competition—which recognises consistency and excellence in design, usability and online experience—this edition reinterprets the historic trophy through a **brutalist aesthetic** defined by simple geometries, visual strength and a clear expression of materiality.

![annual awards trophy](https://assets.awwwards.com/awards/gallery/2026/01/aat-cs-image01.jpg)

The piece is manufactured by [Sustain Awards](https://sustainawards.com/en/), a European design-focused trophy manufacturer specialised in sustainable materials and bespoke production for high-profile international competitions.

A **cement and concrete blend with recycled rubber** aggregate combines mineral density with a subtle elastic texture, resulting in an object with strong tactile character and visual weight, while maintaining a clear commitment to circular design principles.

![annual awards trophy](https://assets.awwwards.com/awards/gallery/2026/01/aat-cs-image04.jpg)

The design preserves the iconic morphology of the Awwwards trophy and introduces a single **point of contrast**: a front-facing “W” with a candy gold mirror finish. This refined detail stands out against the mineral base, reinforcing the competition’s visual identity and acting as a focal point within an otherwise monolithic composition.

![annual awards trophy](https://assets.awwwards.com/awards/gallery/2026/01/aat-cs-image03.jpg)

The result is a **solid, tactile and expressive** object that reflects the same principles celebrated online by Awwwards: clarity, structural coherence and functional beauty. A trophy that not only symbolises digital excellence, but materialises it through responsible manufacturing, design integrity and sustainable innovation led by [Sustain Awards](https://sustainawards.com/en/).

## Jun 7, 2019

# Brutalism: BrutAl wEbsIteS for mOdern dAy webMAsTeRS.

- [![](https://assets.awwwards.com/awards/media/cache/thumb_user_70/avatar/57289/620f55adbdb42025477900.png)**awwwards.**](https://www.awwwards.com/awwwards/)


![Brutalism: BrutAl wEbsIteS for mOdern dAy webMAsTeRS.](https://assets.awwwards.com/awards/images/2017/10/final-COVER.jpg)

Warning designers, we are facing an invasion. A “Brutal” invasion of gigantic cursors, crazy hovers and unusable galleries! Brutalism in web design laughs in the face of rationalism and functionality, in the world of design it can be defined as FreestyleUGLYirreverentRAW and superficially decorative etc. Check out some articles and [examples](http://brutalistwebsites.com/) of brutalist websites that will blow your mind with interesting digressions like [THIS ONE](https://codepen.io/brianhaferkamp/pen/BLdgjk) or [THAT ONE](https://codepen.io/brianhaferkamp/post/brutalism-isn-t-the-absence-of-modernity).

## **How do you spot a** **Brutalist website?**

Coming from the universe of UX & Interaction Designers, (previously known as web designers or even further back [webmasters](https://justinjackson.ca/webmaster/)) We can find an excessive abundance of [Hover effects](https://www.awwwards.com/awwwards/collections/hovers-cursors-and-cute-interactions/), System Fonts, [Web Safe Colors](https://www.w3schools.com/colors/colors_names.asp), Hero Typography, [Micro-Interactions](https://www.awwwards.com/awwwards/collections/animation/), Pop Culture Icons and [Images in the weirdest and most uncomfortable of places.](http://www.xxix.co/) Photos that appear and disappear as if they came with no opacity transition and a long series of user UNfriendly practices, presented here in this article or found in our collection [“Brutalism in Web design”](https://www.awwwards.com/awwwards/collections/brutalism/)
